name: MoE Architectures

name: Mechanistic Interpretability
what: Reverse-engineering the internal circuits of neural networks to understand how specific behaviours arise, instead of treating models as black boxes.
how: Researchers isolate features and circuits inside model activations using sparse autoencoders and causal interventions to map computation.
use: AI safety, debugging model behaviour, detecting deception

name: Neurosymbolic Reasoning
what: Systems that combine neural pattern recognition with symbolic logic to produce reliable, verifiable multi-step reasoning.
how: A neural model proposes candidate steps that a symbolic engine checks against formal rules, looping until the result is provably consistent.

name: Test-Time Training & Continual Learning
what: Models that keep adapting their weights from the data they see at inference time, instead of staying frozen after training.
how: A lightweight self-supervised objective updates a subset of parameters on each new input or task before producing an output.

name: Photonic AI Accelerators
what: Chips that run neural-network matrix math with light instead of electrons, aiming to cut the energy cost of inference.
how: Silicon-photonic meshes perform multiply-accumulate operations optically as light passes through tunable interferometers, with electronics only at the edges.
use: Low-energy datacentre inference, high-throughput linear algebra

name: Analog In-Memory Computing
what: Performing AI computation directly inside memory arrays to avoid shuttling weights back and forth to a separate processor.
how: Analog crossbar arrays (phase-change or resistive memory) compute matrix-vector products in place using device physics rather than digital logic.
